title: NIS2 Incident Detection and 24-Hour Early Warning Reporting

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.23 (Reporting obligations) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.1: NIS2 Incident Detection and 24-Hour Early Warning Reporting.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.1' running on notable macro (rule_name, urgency, status, owner, status_description, _time), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-3d
| eval hours_open=round((now()-_time)/3600, 2)
| eval t_minus_4h=if(hours_open>=20 AND hours_open<24 AND status!="Closed", 1, 0)
| eval past_24h_open=if(hours_open>24 AND status!="Closed", 1, 0)
| table _time, rule_name, urgency, status, owner, status_description, hours_open, t_minus_4h, past_24h_open
| where t_minus_4h=1 OR past_24h_open=1
| sort - past_24h_open, - hours_open
description: Measures detection-to-response progress on high-urgency ES notables to support early-warning obligations and internal crisis reporting within the first 24 hours of awareness.
value: Measures detection-to-response progress on high-urgency ES notables to support early-warning obligations and internal crisis reporting within the first 24 hours of awareness.
implementation: (1) Map ES `urgency` values to your NIS2 incident classes; (2) require analysts to transition `status`/`status_description` at acknowledgement and containment; (3) alert on `t_minus_4h` for CSIRT/legal escalation; (4) export `past_24h_open` rows into crisis-management runbooks and regulatory reporting drafts.
visualization: Timeline (notable aging), Table (stale high-urgency items), Single value (count approaching 24h).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: Measures detection-to-response progress on high-urgency ES notables to support early-warning obligations and internal crisis reporting within the first 24 hours of awareness — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` macro (rule_name, urgency, status, owner, status_description, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map ES `urgency` values to your NIS2 incident classes; (2) require analysts to transition `status`/`status_description` at acknowledgement and containment; (3) alert on `t_minus_4h` for CSIRT/legal escalation; (4) export `past_24h_open` rows into crisis-management runbooks and regulatory reporting drafts.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-3d
| eval hours_open=round((now()-_time)/3600, 2)
| eval t_minus_4h=if(hours_open>=20 AND hours_open<24 AND status!="Closed", 1, 0)
| eval past_24h_open=if(hours_open>24 AND status!="Closed", 1, 0)
| table _time, rule_name, urgency, status, owner, status_description, hours_open, t_minus_4h, past_24h_open
| where t_minus_4h=1 OR past_24h_open=1
| sort - past_24h_open, - hours_open
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Incident Detection and 24-Hour Early Warning Reporting (Art. 23)** — Measures detection-to-response progress on high-urgency ES notables to support early-warning obligations and internal crisis reporting within the first 24 hours of awareness.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` macro (rule_name, urgency, status, owner, status_description, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_open** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **t_minus_4h** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **past_24h_open** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **NIS2 Incident Detection and 24-Hour Early Warning Reporting (Art. 23)**): table _time, rule_name, urgency, status, owner, status_description, hours_open, t_minus_4h, past_24h_open
• Filters the current rows with `where t_minus_4h=1 OR past_24h_open=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (notable aging), Table (stale high-urgency items), Single value (count approaching 24h).
